name: AKARI Keymaster
description: Read-only credential agent for autonomous AI systems. Exposes Vault-backed secret retrieval, service discovery, and credential health checks via A2A, without granting write access to the secret store.

keymasterBearer.description: Per-agent Bearer token issued from Keymaster admin. Scoped read-only. No Vault write access is granted.

skills.name: Get Secret
skills.description: Fetch an approved API credential from Vault through the read-only Keymaster boundary. The calling agent receives the secret value at runtime — it is never stored in the agent's config or memory.

skills.name: List Services
skills.description: Enumerate all service/key-name pairs registered in Vault, and whether each key can be verified against its upstream API. Useful for workflow planning before a job starts.

skills.name: List Secrets
skills.description: Enumerate all retrievable secret paths in service/key_name form. Suitable for audit-safe discovery before a multi-step pipeline.

skills.name: Health Check
skills.description: Validate all known credentials against their upstream service APIs before agents begin critical work. Returns a structured report with valid/invalid/error counts.

skills.name: Rotate Secret (Guidance)
skills.description: When a secret must be changed, returns the safe Vault-side rotation path instead of performing a write. This agent is intentionally read-only — rotation is performed by a human or a dedicated privileged agent directly in Vault.

examples.output: Rotation path: vault kv put akarihearts-v2/api_keys/stripe secret_key=<new_value>

authenticationNote: This agent is read-only by design. The Bearer token grants scoped read access to approved Vault paths only. No Vault write operations are exposed. Rotation is handled out-of-band via Vault UI/CLI/API by a human or privileged agent.
